concerns.text: T030 and T031 introduce 'topological attractor analysis' (RQA) and a 'Phase Transition Analysis' section. These deliverables are not present in FR-003 through FR-006, nor in the spec's Success Criteria. This constitutes unapproved scope creep that violates the spec's defined functional requirements and the plan's conditional analysis logic.

T022 is marked as rejected/missing, yet it is the sole task responsible for generating the final report mandated by FR-004 (correlation coefficients, p-values, CIs). The task list fails to provide a path to a verified deliverable for this critical FR, effectively dropping the acceptance criteria for the final report.

T014 and T015 are marked as rejected/missing. These are the only tasks assigned to implement FR-003 (calculate Lempel-Ziv complexity and permutation entropy). Without a re-plan or verified implementation, the spec's core requirement for complexity metrics has no executable task to deliver it.

T010 is marked as rejected. It is the sole task assigned to implement FR-002 (preprocess with 1–40 Hz bandpass and 50 Hz notch filter). The missing implementation leaves the spec's mandatory signal processing pipeline without a verified execution step.

T027 is marked as rejected. It is the only task addressing the 'Edge Cases' section of spec.md (missing data, analysis mode failures) and the validation logic in T009/T018. The rejection of this task drops the acceptance criteria for error handling and system robustness.

T001 is marked as rejected. It is the foundational task for project structure required by plan.md and Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene). Its rejection blocks the verification of the project layout before any code can be written.

T018 implements a conditional fallback (paired vs. cross-sectional) but the plan.md Summary introduces an 'ANCOVA approach (Post ~ Pre + Fatigue)' for paired data which contradicts FR-004's strict mandate for 'Pearson or Spearman correlation between complexity changes (delta) and fatigue delta scores'. No task explicitly implements the ANCOVA model, leaving the plan's approach untraced and potentially violating the spec's statistical method.
